pick.rationale: Howard edges UMBC in this tightly contested First Four matchup, leveraging their superior defensive efficiency and significant motivation to secure their first-ever NCAA Tournament victory. While UMBC holds advantages in overall efficiency rankings and offensive output, Howard's recent tournament experience under coach Kenneth Blakeney and their MEAC Tournament championship momentum prove decisive. The Bison's ability to get stops in critical moments, combined with their historic opportunity to check the final box on their tournament resume, allows them to prevail in a close contest.

reasoningStep.title: Howard vs UMBC First Four Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Howard defeats UMBC in the Midwest First Four, driven by defensive advantage and tournament motivation despite UMBC's superior efficiency rankings.
reasoningStep.evidence: KenPom ratings show UMBC ranked 185th vs Howard 207th, but Howard has better defensive efficiency (106.4 vs 109.9 adj_d). Howard won the MEAC Tournament on March 14, while UMBC won the America East Tournament the same day. Multiple sources confirm Howard's 86-83 victory marks their first NCAA Tournament win ever. UMBC's first NCAA appearance since 2018 lacked the same recent tournament experience as Howard's third appearance in four years under Blakeney.
